title: Progressive upscale
completionRule: Complete only when nearby and dwell-triggered progressive upscale preserve every source control and state, share one policy/page/resource pipeline across GUI/CLI/TUI, cancel stale generations, expose bounded progress, and use a documented bounded artifact cache instead of the legacy unbounded Blob map.

sourceUiInventory.title: Automatic and nearby-upscale gates
acceptanceItems: Switch disabled when automatic upscale is disabled
Warning panel when automatic upscale is disabled

sourceUiInventory.title: Nearby range and concurrency options
acceptanceItems: Controls hidden unless nearby and automatic upscale are enabled

sourceUiInventory.title: Dwell-triggered progressive options
acceptanceItems: 999 displayed and interpreted as all remaining pages
Controls hidden unless progressive and automatic upscale are enabled

sourceUiInventory.title: Completed, queued and total progress
acceptanceItems: Bounded task outcomes for settled, skipped, failed and cancelled pages

sourceUiInventory.title: Progressive timer and execution states
acceptanceItems: Running state with animated cyan indicator
Countdown state with amber indicator and seconds
No duplicate dwell timer after setting changes

sourceUiInventory.title: Navigation, cancellation and disposal lifecycle
acceptanceItems: Page change resets the dwell timer
New generation cancels stale nearby work
New generation cancels stale progressive work
Card unmount clears dwell and countdown timers
Reader session close releases all context work
Current-page and view GPU work retain priority over background upscale
Observer failure cannot change task outcome

items.title: Versioned settings, defaults and legacy import
items.targetContract: All six switches/numeric settings use the versioned [nodes.neoview.super_resolution.preferences] contract, preserve legacy defaults and option meanings, and import old upscalePanelSettings/PyO3 values without a second runtime settings store.
items.notes: Core parsing and legacy import exist; shared mutation transport and presentation controls remain.

items.title: Nearby pages consume the Reader preload plan
items.targetContract: Select unique nearby pages in the existing ReaderPreloadPlan order, cap the batch by preloadPages, preserve candidate resource priority and execute through the one SuperResolutionPageService.
items.notes: Application service is complete; artifact destination and Reader session transport remain.

items.title: Abortable dwell before progressive execution
items.targetContract: Use one abortable dwell per session generation; navigation, disable, close and disposal supersede the old timer without retaining intervals or starting stale GPU work.
items.notes: Uses node:timers/promises with AbortSignal; countdown presentation remains.

items.title: Forward-only progressive page bound
items.targetContract: After dwell, process pages strictly after the current index in stable book order; honor progressiveMaxPages and preserve legacy 999=all-remaining semantics.
items.notes: No reverse expansion or wrap-around is introduced.

items.title: Session generation supersession
items.targetContract: Nearby and progressive work use separate session-scoped generation slots; a newer generation aborts stale work, an older generation is rejected and same-generation callers share the active batch.
items.notes: The service does not maintain a second page queue; p-map bounds only the current selected batch.

items.title: Bounded concurrency and host resource priority
items.targetContract: Use mature p-map for configured 1..32 batch concurrency, then retain the existing SuperResolutionService GPU lease, page materializer I/O lease and Reader candidate priorities; never create another worker pool or process queue.
items.notes: backgroundConcurrency is an admission bound; the host scheduler remains the final global resource authority.

items.title: Bounded progress and outcome snapshot
items.targetContract: Expose planned, settled, failed and cancelled counts plus per-page settled/skipped/failed/cancelled outcomes; observer failures are isolated and recent state is bounded by the active Reader generation.
items.notes: Batch result exists; a shared live snapshot with pause/retry controls is not yet exposed.

items.title: Shared destination and page-result contract
items.targetContract: Platform code supplies an artifact destination without creating it; the application service passes book/page metadata, generation, priority, materialization budget and AbortSignal to the canonical page workflow and returns typed outcomes.
items.notes: The canonical page policy now plans once before resolving the opaque artifact fingerprint; preload can warm the injected streaming store without exposing a raw destination. The authenticated opaque HTTP asset remains pending.

items.title: Bounded upscale artifact cache
items.targetContract: Replace the legacy unbounded in-memory Blob map and PyO3 cache with one streaming, byte-budgeted artifact store supporting fingerprint invalidation, current-book/age/all cleanup, stats and opaque asset leases without reading high-resolution outputs wholly into JS memory.
items.notes: A dedicated cacache streaming store now provides byte/age/book/all maintenance, integrity validation, atomic publication, demand-counted singleflight and opaque read leases. It remains intentionally separate from the buffering presentation cache; user-facing controls and HTTP delivery remain pending.

items.title: Open, navigation, release and dispose lifecycle
items.targetContract: Start only after an enabled plan/dwell, cancel on navigation or session release, await active work during dispose, release archive inputs and provider processes, and leave zero timers/tasks after the Reader closes.
items.notes: Application disposal exists; Reader session composition is still pending.

items.title: Card hierarchy, density and state parity
items.targetContract: Preserve the source hierarchy, compact selects, warning, dividers, counts, progress bar, colored status indicators and conditional visibility in docked, settings-window and card-window layouts using XR tokens.
items.notes: No React Card has been implemented.

items.title: Keyboard and assistive semantics
items.targetContract: All switches and selects have programmatic labels, predictable focus order and keyboard operation; status changes are announced without duplicating timers. The source Card defines no dedicated shortcut, so XR must not invent a conflicting global binding.
items.notes: Keyboard equivalence is required even though the old Card has no dedicated shortcut.

items.title: Documented scheduler and cache replacements
items.targetContract: Record that XR replaces the source hand-written task queue, interval countdown ownership and unbounded Blob Map with ReaderPreloadPlan, p-map, abortable timers, host resource leases and a streaming artifact store while preserving user-visible options and states.
items.notes: The backend scheduler and cache replacements are complete and covered by repeatable tests; transport and user-visible parity remain separate pending surfaces.
